title: prunus domestica var. sylvestris
intro: Prunus domestica var. sylvestris, also known as wild plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Prunus genus. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, diarrhea, and fever. The fruit of the wild plum tree is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Wild plum is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusional in herbal medicine, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
